title: Cancer Navigator (Serenity)
description:
Job Description & Requirements

Essential Duties and Responsibilities:

The core duty of the Cancer Navigator is to provide care and support for adult cancer patients through psycho-social and emotional support which contribute to patient’s overall well-being and improved quality of life.

  • Provide assistance for clients depending on their needs (newly diagnosed, actively receiving cancer treatment, palliative/end-of-life, survivorship)
  • Provide psycho-social and emotional support for clients and their caregivers
  • Link clients with relevant and suitable resources and services (such as financial assistance, food rations, transportation, mobility equipment, medical consumables etc.)
  • Follow up with community partners to ensure continuity of care after referrals
  • Refer client for counselling, and spiritual or mental health support as and when the needs arise
  • Provide information or coordinate for clients to receive psychoeducation from medical/healthcare practitioners which could help them have better understanding of the cancer diagnosis, and its treatments and side effects
  • Maintain relationship and communicate frequently with clients and caregivers to assess their coping as they navigate their cancer journey
  • Advocate for the needs of cancer patients and create awareness on cancer through community programs and publicity campaigns
  • Document case recordings according to interventions provided for the client
  • Carry out assigned administrative and coordinating duties required to run programs and support group
  • Contribute to planning and running of programs and support groups, depending on the needs of cancer patients in the centre
  • Faciliate support group session and programs for cancer patients and their caregivers in the centre

Education and/or Work Experience Requirements:

  • Degree in Counselling, Psychology
  • Demonstrated experience in case management and supporting people impacted by illnesses/multiple adversities
  • Skills in demonstrating warmth and empathy to individuals impacted by illnesses/multiple adversities
  • Interest in providing support for individuals/families from the lower socio-economic status
  • Open to work with clients of various age groups (children, adolescents, young adults, middle adults, older adults)

Other Requirements:

  • Able to coordinate logistical requirements for in-house and external programmes
  • Able to work on weekends as required by supervisor if the need arises (for outings with clients, support group sessions, outreach programmes)
  • Ability to travel for home-visits

postedCompany.description:
Ain Society is a social service agency registered with the Registry of Societies since Oct 2000, with an IPC status. We are affiliated to the National Council of Social Service.

Our Medical Advisor is Professor Fatimah Lateef (former Member of Parliament for Marine Parade GRC), Senior Consultant, Director of Undergraduate Training and Education as well as Director of Clinical Service and Quality at the Department of Emergency Medicine, Singapore General Hospital.

Since its establishment in 2000, Ain Society has made a significant impact, positively affecting the lives of over 45,000 individuals through a range of developmental, remedial, and rehabilitative programs. In our years of providing social service, we found that many of our clients are either cancer patients or have family members afflicted with cancer. It is alarming to note that from 2017-2021 an average of 46 people were diagnosed with cancer and 16 people died of cancer every day, and it is the leading cause of death in Singapore.

Presently, the organization's primary focus lies in providing support to individuals and families affected by cancer, irrespective of their race or religion. This assistance encompasses moral, emotional, social, and financial aid. Two dedicated centers are operational for this purpose: one at Blk 2 Eunos Crescent, catering to children with cancer (Young Heroes Cancer Care), and another at Blk 9 Haig Road, catering to adults with cancer (Serenity Cancer Care).

Recognizing the unique needs of adolescents and young adults (AYA) diagnosed with cancer, Ain Society has recently initiated the Adolescents and Young Adult (AYA) program. This progam aims to bridge the gap for this specific age group, who fall between being too mature for the children's center and too young for the adult cancer center. Prior to this initiative, no agency had addressed the niche requirements of this clientele. In response to this gap, the organization is expanding its services at the current center located at Blk 2 Eunos Crescent, specifically for the AYA program.
